questions.question: What is the origin of the word 'yoga'?
questions.explanation: The Sanskrit root 'yuj' means 'to join' or 'to unite'

questions.question: What does the symbol of yoga represent?
Who presented the ancient tradition of yoga in a systematic way?
What is one benefit of yoga postures (asanas)?
Who launched the WHO mYoga App?
What is yoga's effect on blood pressure?
How does yoga aid in weight loss?
What is the reason for the benefits of practising yoga being internationally recognised?
questions.explanation: Internationally recognised by WHO and others

questions.question: When was the WHO mYoga App launched?

notes.title: Origin of Yoga
notes.content: The word 'yoga' comes from the Sanskrit root 'yuj', which means 'to join' or 'to unite'

notes.title: Symbolism of Yoga
notes.content: Represents unity of mind and body, harmony between human and nature

notes.title: Sage Patanjali's Contribution
notes.content: Presented the ancient tradition of yoga in a systematic way in 'Yoga Sutras of Patanjali'

notes.title: Benefits of Yoga Postures
notes.content: Stretch and strengthen muscles, increase flexibility, endurance, and balance

notes.title: Reducing Stress with Yoga
notes.content: Meditation helps calm the mind, reduce stress and anxiety

notes.title: WHO mYoga App
notes.content: Launched by World Health Organisation (WHO) on 20 June 2021

notes.title: Effect of Yoga on Blood Pressure
Yoga and Weight Loss
notes.content: Aids in weight loss by burning calories, maintaining health

notes.title: International Recognition of Yoga Benefits
notes.content: Benefits of practising yoga are internationally recognised due to promoting overall well-being

notes.title: Holistic Approach to Health and Well-being
notes.content: Yoga is a holistic approach to health and well-being, balancing body, mind, and spirit

facts.fact: The word 'yoga' comes from the Sanskrit root 'yuj', which means 'to join' or 'to unite'
Sage Patanjali presented the ancient tradition of yoga in a systematic way in 'Yoga Sutras of Patanjali'
Yoga postures (asanas) help to stretch and strengthen muscles, increase flexibility, endurance, and balance
Meditation helps calm the mind, reduce stress and anxiety
The WHO mYoga App was launched by World Health Organisation (WHO) on 20 June 2021
Yoga can aid in weight loss by burning calories, maintaining health
The benefits of practising yoga are internationally recognised due to promoting overall well-being
Yoga is a holistic approach to health and well-being, balancing body, mind, and spirit
Yoga can improve heart health by lowering blood pressure, improving circulation
The World Health Organisation (WHO) recognises the benefits of yoga for overall well-being
